message.content:
You are an independent, adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer A) for the hc-assay hardening campaign at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-9, latest commit). Confirm the fix is BOTH appropriate (correct root cause, sound approach, right layer) AND complete (no residual gap; regression test DISCRIMINATES — fails if reverted). Be skeptical; find holes. For a constrained finding (CI-1) judge the response given the real constraint.

FINDING P9-PII-1:
Operator machine/namespace hostname leaked in committed transcripts ([REDACTED][.local], [REDACTED]), no scrub rule. Fix in scripts/capture_transcripts.py: _hostname_patterns() derives the MACHINE hostname (socket.gethostname/platform.node/scutil) → redacts bare + ".local"; ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS entries → redact ONLY the ".local" form (bare project token like hc-grc preserved). Applied in _scrub. The committed corpus was re-scrubbed. VERIFY: grep transcripts/ for [REDACTED] / [REDACTED] / [REDACTED] → 0; bare 'hc-grc' still present (not over-redacted); unrelated 'settings.local.json' untouched. Test: test_scrub_redacts_machine_and_namespace_hostnames. Check the derived machine pattern doesn't over-redact a short/common hostname.

Read the changed files and run what you can (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path>; "python -m pytest" not bare pytest; gh available for CI-1). Judge ONLY P9-PII-1. CONFIRMED only if appropriate AND complete; CONCERN if a real residual gap; REJECTED if wrong/non-discriminating. List concrete concerns (empty if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll review finding P9-PII-1. Let me start by examining the changed files.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git log --oneline -5 && echo "---" && git show --stat HEAD
input.description: Show recent commits and latest change stats

8c5d7e5 fix(hardening-p9): 5 confirmed incl. security regression of pass-8 unix-socket fix
6e3d6b4 Merge pull request #169 from [REDACTED]/harden/pass-8
71ed4d5 fix(hardening-p8): CI follow-ons surfaced by actually running CI (K-CI-3 + lockfile via-comments)
23de0f6 docs(hardening): record pass 8 — 8 confirmed incl. 3 CI-red blockers; main CI was failing
9998558 fix(hardening-p8): strengthen coverage-floor guard (K-OPS-3 confirm-concern)
---
commit 8c5d7e5268db467a3918f84ebcb9573cfef3334f
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 12:29:53 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p9): 5 confirmed incl. security regression of pass-8 unix-socket fix
    
    All 2-agent verified TRUE_POSITIVE.
    
    - K-SEC-9-1 (high, SECURITY REGRESSION of pass-8 K-SEC-1): the Unix-socket allowance
      checked the whole host string, so libpq's comma multi-host 'host=/tmp,evil.com' was
      ACCEPTED (libpq tries the socket then falls back to the off-box TCP host → exfiltration
      past ADR-0003). Fix: is_local_socket_path rejects comma values; the DSN+query branches
      now SPLIT on ',' and validate every element (mirroring the authority branch). Verified:
      all multi-host bypasses rejected, legit single socket/loopback accepted.
    - CI-1 (high): branch protection is unavailable on this plan (private/free → 403), so the
      all-checks gate is unenforced — the mechanism behind the campaign's silently-red CI.
      Enabling it is out of scope (public repo would publish transcripts; plan upgrade is a
      billing decision). Compensating control: scripts/require_green_ci.sh (pre-merge gate),
      docs/MERGE-POLICY.md, and an honest all-checks caveat comment.
    - P9-PII-1 (med): operator machine/namespace hostname ([REDACTED][.local], [REDACTED])
      leaked into committed transcripts — no scrub rule covered it. Fix: _hostname_patterns
      derives the machine hostname (socket/platform/scutil) → redacts bare + .local; an
      ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS namespace host has only its .local form redacted (bare project token
      preserved; unrelated *.local untouched). Re-scrubbed corpus (grep now 0).
    - P9-MO-1 (med): confirm primitives raised an opaque TypeError when alpha was locked on
      neither hypothesis nor call; now a ValueError naming alpha and how to provide it.
    - P9-MO-2 (low): bare int()/float() env parsing in seam.py/checkpoint.py raised opaque
      errors naming no var. Fix: shared assay_engine._envparse (int_env/float_env) naming the
      var, wired into seam.py + checkpoint.py.
    
    Regression tests for every finding. Gates: ruff + format + mypy --strict clean; 563
    passed, 5 skipped; coverage floor enforced.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Capture the active Claude session's JSONL transcript(s) into the repo for provenance.
3	
4	Run by the pre-commit hook (.githooks/pre-commit) so every commit pulls in an up-to-date
5	snapshot of the agent interactions/effort behind it — an auditable record alongside the work.
6	
7	What it captures: the *active* session (the most-recently-modified top-level transcript in the
8	Claude project dir) plus that session's ``<id>/`` subtree (subagents + workflow transcripts).
9	Other/unrelated sessions are left out. Credential-shaped secrets are redacted on the way in.
10	
11	Honest scope: transcripts still contain local filesystem paths and may contain the operator's
12	email and internal reasoning. If this repo is published, review/exclude ``transcripts/`` first
13	(see transcripts/README.md). The wheel never ships them (only ``src/assay_engine`` is packaged).
14	
15	Config: ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC overrides the source dir. Exits 0 on any error (never blocks a
16	commit).
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import datetime as _dt
22	import hashlib
23	import json
24	import os
25	import platform
26	import re
27	import socket
28	import sys
29	from pathlib import Path
30	
31	DEFAULT_SRC = Path.home() / ".claude" / "projects" / "-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc"
32	DEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "sessions"
33	MANIFEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "MANIFEST.json"
34	
35	# Credential-shaped secrets to redact (defense). Pass 3 (#F-024) extends this to operator PII —
36	# email addresses and the home-directory username embedded in filesystem paths — so committing
37	# transcripts as provenance (the campaign mandate) does not also publish the operator's identity.
38	# Reasoning content and the path STRUCTURE remain (legitimate provenance); see the README caveat
39	# for the full public-release consideration.
40	_SECRET_PATTERNS = [
41	    re.compile(r"sk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2,}"),  # any sk-ant- token incl. oat01 OAuth + placeholders
42	    re.compile(r"\bgh[posru]_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b"),  # GitHub tokens
43	    re.compile(r"\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b"),  # AWS access key id
44	    # bearer tokens incl. base64url/base64 JWTs — must cover + / = or the token is split at the
45	    # first such char and only the leading fragment is redacted (pass 3, #F-051).
46	    re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-+/=]{20,}"),
47	    # explicit OAuth/token env values: "...OAUTH_TOKEN":"<value>" / ...KEY=<value>
48	    re.compile(r'((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)"\s*:\s*")[^"]{8,}(")'),
49	    re.compile(r"((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)=)[^\s\"']{8,}"),
50	    # operator PII (#F-024): email addresses, and the username segment of a home path
51	    # (/Users/[REDACTED] /home/[REDACTED] — the path STRUCTURE is kept, only the identity is masked.
52	    re.compile(r"\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b"),  # email → [REDACTED]
53	    re.compile(r"(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s\"']+"),  # /Users/[REDACTED] → /Users/[REDACTED]    # The Claude project-dir SLUG (e.g. -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc): redact the username segment
55	    # regardless of length (#H-018) — the bare-token rule below skips short handles to avoid
56	    # over-redacting common words, but the slug form is unambiguous PII at any length.
57	    re.compile(r"(-(?:Users|home)-)[^-\s/\"']+"),  # -Users-[REDACTED]- → -Users-[REDACTED]-
58	]
59	
60	
61	def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
62	    """A redaction for the bare operator username wherever it appears (#G-007).
63	
64	    The /Users|/home path rule only masks the username inside a path; the same handle leaks via
65	    git author strings, prose, and the Claude project-dir slug (``-Users-[REDACTED]-...``). Derive the
66	    handle from the home directory and redact it as a standalone token too. Skipped for trivially
67	    short/None handles (e.g. a CI 'root') to avoid over-redacting common words.
68	    """
69	    try:
70	        name = Path.home().name
71	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never break capture over a missing home
72	        return None
73	    if not name or len(name) < 4:
74	        return None
75	    return re.compile(re.escape(name))
76	
77	
78	def _display_name_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
79	    """Redactions for the operator's full DISPLAY NAME (#J-002).
80	
81	    The username/email rules leave the real name (e.g. a git ``user.name`` like "[REDACTED]")
82	    in cleartext — distinct operator PII that appears in git author strings, ``pyproject``
83	    ``authors=``, and prose, often right beside an already-redacted email. Derive candidate names
84	    from ``git config user.name`` and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES`` env (comma-separated), redact each
85	    (and, for a multi-word name, its whitespace-flexible form). Length-guarded to avoid
86	    over-redacting common words.
87	    """
88	    names: list[str] = []
89	    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "")
90	    names.extend(n.strip() for n in extra.split(",") if n.strip())
91	    # Derive the operator name from SEVERAL sources — any one may be empty in a given context
92	    # (#J-002 follow-up): on this node local `git config user.name` is unset yet commits still
93	    # carry an author via global config / env, so a single-source lookup scrubbed nothing. Cover
94	    # the env author vars, git config, AND the actual last-commit author.
95	    for env_name in ("GIT_AUTHOR_NAME", "GIT_COMMITTER_NAME"):
96	        v = os.environ.get(env_name, "").strip()
97	        if v:
98	            names.append(v)
99	    try:
100	        import subprocess
101	
102	        for cmd in (["git", "config", "user.name"], ["git", "log", "-1", "--format=%an"]):
103	            out = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
104	            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
105	                names.append(out.stdout.strip())
106	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
107	        pass
108	    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
109	    seen: set[str] = set()
110	    for n in names:
111	        if len(n) < 4 or n in seen:
112	            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words; or already added
113	        seen.add(n)
114	        # Flexible inter-token whitespace (e.g. "[REDACTED]"). NO leading \b: the name often
115	        # appears glued to a JSON-escaped newline ("...---\\n[REDACTED]") where \b doesn't fire
116	        # because the escape's 'n' abuts the name (#J-002 follow-up). A trailing \b still prevents
117	        # matching inside a longer word (e.g. "Joneses"); the >=4-char guard bounds over-redaction.
118	        # CASE-INSENSITIVE (#J-002 confirm-concern round 2): the operator's name also appears
119	        # lowercased as actor/handle tokens — "[REDACTED]@hcgrc" (an initiated_by field), "[REDACTED]-[REDACTED]
120	        # (human)" in actor tables — which a case-sensitive pattern misses. re.IGNORECASE covers all
121	        # casings; "Joneses"/"joneses" stays protected by the trailing \b either way.
122	        toks = n.split()
123	        pats.append(re.compile(r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in toks) + r"\b", re.IGNORECASE))
124	        # Redact each NAME TOKEN standalone too (#J-002 confirm-concern): the full-name pattern
125	        # leaves the operator's bare first/last name in cleartext — e.g. "[REDACTED] approves ..." and
126	        # "[REDACTED] builds and governs ..." appear in governance prose hundreds of times, uniquely
127	        # re-identifying the operator. Per-token redaction (len>=4, trailing \b, no leading \b for
128	        # the JSON-\n-glued case) closes it. Over-redaction is bounded: the tokens come only from
129	        # the verified operator name and the corpus contains no unrelated bearer of these tokens
130	        # (all 256 "[REDACTED]" contexts were sampled — every one is an operator governance reference).
131	        for t in toks:
132	            if len(t) >= 4 and t.lower() not in seen:
133	                seen.add(t.lower())
134	                pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(t) + r"\b", re.IGNORECASE))
135	    return pats
136	
137	
138	def _repo_handle_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
139	    """Redactions for the operator's repo owner / GitHub handle (#J-008).
140	
141	    The handle (the ``<owner>`` in ``github.com/<owner>/<repo>``, e.g. in PR/issue URLs and
142	    ``gh --repo <owner>/...`` commands) is operator-identifying PII that NO existing rule covers:
143	    it is not an email (the email rule needs an ``@``), and it differs from the home-dir username
144	    the bare-username rule derives. Left uncovered it leaked across hundreds of transcript files
145	    (#J-008). Derive candidate handles from the git remotes and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_HANDLES`` env;
146	    redact each as a bare token (length-guarded). A handle is a single unambiguous string, so no
147	    word-boundary is needed — redact it inside URLs, ``gh`` args, and the ``<handle>@`` email stem.
148	    Matched case-insensitively, and the distinctive STEM (handle minus a generic ``the`` prefix) is
149	    also redacted, because the handle leaks via derived names — e.g. ``[REDACTED]-audience-profiles``
150	    (a skill name) embeds the handle minus ``the`` (#J-008 confirm-concern).
151	    """
152	    handles: list[str] = []
153	    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_HANDLES", "")
154	    handles.extend(h.strip() for h in extra.split(",") if h.strip())
155	    try:
156	        import subprocess
157	
158	        out = subprocess.run(["git", "remote", "-v"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
159	        if out.returncode == 0:
160	            for m in re.finditer(r"github\.com[:/]([^/\s]+)/", out.stdout):
161	                handles.append(m.group(1))
162	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
163	        pass
164	    # Expand each handle with its distinctive stem (drop a leading generic "the") so handle-derived
165	    # names/slugs are covered too (#J-008 confirm-concern).
166	    expanded: list[str] = []
167	    for h in handles:
168	        expanded.append(h)
169	        if h.lower().startswith("the") and len(h) - 3 >= 4:
170	            expanded.append(h[3:])
171	    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
172	    seen: set[str] = set()
173	    for h in expanded:
174	        key = h.lower()
175	        if len(h) < 4 or key in seen:
176	            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words; or already added
177	        seen.add(key)
178	        if len(h) >= 8:
179	            # SEPARATOR-FLEXIBLE (#J-008 confirm-concern round 3): a GitHub handle is usually a
180	            # de-spaced brand ("[REDACTED]" = "[REDACTED]"); the SPACED source brand
181	            # appears in prose/links and lets a reader reconstruct the redacted handle/email by
182	            # concatenation. Allow punctuation/whitespace between each character so the spaced,
183	            # hyphenated, and dotted forms all redact — while the de-spaced form (empty separators)
184	            # still matches. The separator class is space/._- ONLY, so unrelated prose cannot
185	            # accidentally spell out the handle, and common single tokens (e.g. "CISO") are NOT
186	            # redacted because the WHOLE letter sequence (…[REDACTED]) must be present in order.
187	            pats.append(re.compile(r"[\s._-]*".join(re.escape(c) for c in h), re.IGNORECASE))
188	        else:
189	            pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(h), re.IGNORECASE))
190	    return pats
191	
192	
193	def _hostname_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
194	    """Redactions for the operator's machine/infrastructure hostname (#P9-MO/P9-PII-1).
195	
196	    The scrubber masked email/username/display-name/handle but had NO rule for the host the node
197	    runs on, so the production machine's hostname (e.g. ``[REDACTED]`` / ``[REDACTED]``) and
198	    internal ``*.local`` mDNS/namespace hosts leaked into committed transcripts — operator/infra
199	    identity. Derive the MACHINE hostname from ``socket.gethostname``/``platform.node`` (and, on
200	    macOS, ``scutil --get LocalHostName``/``ComputerName``) and redact both its bare and ``.local``
201	    forms (a machine name is distinctive). Additional internal hosts (e.g. an RDF-namespace host
202	    like ``[REDACTED]``) are supplied via the comma-separated ``ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS`` env and have
203	    ONLY their ``.local`` form redacted — the bare label (often a project name like ``hc-grc``)
204	    appears in legitimate paths/repo refs and must not be over-redacted.
205	    """
206	    machine: list[str] = []
207	    try:
208	        machine.append(socket.gethostname())
209	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
210	        pass
211	    try:
212	        machine.append(platform.node())
213	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
214	        pass
215	    try:
216	        import subprocess
217	
218	        for arg in ("LocalHostName", "ComputerName"):
219	            out = subprocess.run(["scutil", "--get", arg], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
220	            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
221	                machine.append(out.stdout.strip())
222	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - scutil absent (non-macOS) must never break capture
223	        pass
224	    extra = [h.strip() for h in os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS", "").split(",") if h.strip()]
225	
226	    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
227	    seen: set[str] = set()
228	
229	    def _base(name: str) -> str:
230	        n = name.strip()
231	        return n[: -len(".local")] if n.lower().endswith(".local") else n
232	
233	    for name in machine:  # machine hostname → redact BARE + .local (distinctive identity)
234	        b = _base(name)
235	        if len(b) < 4 or b.lower() in seen:
236	            continue
237	        seen.add(b.lower())
238	        pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(b) + r"(?:\.local)?\b", re.IGNORECASE))
239	    for name in extra:  # supplied namespace hosts → redact ONLY the .local FQDN form
240	        b = _base(name)
241	        if len(b) < 4 or ("local:" + b.lower()) in seen:
242	            continue
243	        seen.add("local:" + b.lower())
244	        pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(b) + r"\.local\b", re.IGNORECASE))
245	    return pats
246	
247	
248	_USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
249	_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = _display_name_patterns()
250	_REPO_HANDLE_RES = _repo_handle_patterns()
251	_HOSTNAME_RES = _hostname_patterns()
252	
253	
254	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
255	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
256	        if pat.groups == 2:
257	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
258	        elif pat.groups == 1:
259	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
260	        else:
261	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
262	    # Redact the bare operator username everywhere (#G-007), after the path rule so masked paths
263	    # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
264	    if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
265	        text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
266	    # Redact the operator's full display name + bare name tokens too (#J-002) — distinct PII the
267	    # username/email rules leave in cleartext (git author strings, pyproject authors=, prose).
268	    for pat in _DISPLAY_NAME_RES:
269	        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
270	    # Redact the repo owner / GitHub handle (#J-008) — operator-identifying PII in PR/issue URLs
271	    # and `gh --repo <handle>/...` commands that no email/username rule covers.
272	    for pat in _REPO_HANDLE_RES:
273	        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
274	    # Redact the machine/infrastructure hostname (#P9-PII-1) — the production node's hostname and
275	    # internal *.local hosts that no other rule covers.
276	    for pat in _HOSTNAME_RES:
277	        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
278	    return text
279	
280	
281	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
282	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
283	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
284	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
285	
286	
287	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
288	    try:
289	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
290	        raw = srcf.read_bytes()
291	        # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
292	        # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
293	        # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
294	        #
295	        # FAIL CLOSED on redaction (#J-001): classify binary by an actual binary SIGNAL (a NUL
296	        # byte), NOT by UTF-8 validity. A transcript routinely embeds arbitrary subprocess stdout
297	        # (latin-1 dumps, truncated multibyte) inside JSONL strings; gating on strict decode meant
298	        # a single stray byte routed the WHOLE file to the verbatim branch and re-leaked it (the
299	        # CV-O-1 fix had turned an always-redact path into a fail-open one). Decode with
300	        # errors="replace" so redaction ALWAYS runs on text; only a genuinely binary blob (NUL
301	        # present) is copied verbatim.
302	        if b"\x00" in raw:
303	            data = raw  # genuinely binary — nothing to scrub
304	        else:
305	            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
306	        if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
307	            pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
308	        else:
309	            dstf.write_bytes(data)
310	        return {
311	            "path": str(dstf.relative_to(DEST.parent.parent)),
312	            "bytes": len(data),
313	            "sha256": hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest(),
314	        }
315	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — provenance capture must never break a commit
316	        return None
317	
318	
319	def main() -> int:
320	    src = Path(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC", str(DEFAULT_SRC)))
321	    if not src.is_dir():
322	        return 0
323	    session = _active_session(src)
324	    if session is None:
325	        return 0
326	    sid = session.stem
327	    captured: list[dict] = []
328	
329	    rec = _copy_scrubbed(session, DEST / f"{sid}.jsonl")
330	    if rec:
331	        captured.append(rec)
332	
333	    # the session's subtree: subagents/ + workflows/ transcripts
334	    subtree = src / sid
335	    if subtree.is_dir():
336	        for f in sorted(subtree.rglob("*")):
337	            if f.is_file():
338	                rel = f.relative_to(subtree)
339	                rec = _copy_scrubbed(f, DEST / sid / rel)
340	                if rec:
341	                    captured.append(rec)
342	
343	    try:
344	        MANIFEST.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
345	        MANIFEST.write_text(
346	            json.dumps(
347	                {
348	                    "captured_at": _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
349	                    "active_session": sid,
350	                    "n_files": len(captured),
351	                    "total_bytes": sum(c["bytes"] for c in captured),
352	                    "files": captured,
353	                },
354	                indent=2,
355	            )
356	            + "\n",
357	            encoding="utf-8",
358	        )
359	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
360	        return 0
361	    print(f"captured {len(captured)} transcript file(s) for session {sid}")
362	    return 0
363	
364	
365	if __name__ == "__main__":
366	    sys.exit(main())
367

+def test_scrub_redacts_machine_and_namespace_hostnames(monkeypatch):
+    # #P9-PII-1: the production node's hostname and internal *.local hosts are operator/infra PII
+    # that no email/username/handle rule covered. The machine hostname is redacted bare + .local;
+    # an ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS namespace host has its .local form redacted while the bare project token
+    # (which appears in legit paths/repo refs) is preserved, and unrelated "*.local" is untouched.
+    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS", "hc-proj")
+    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_host")
+    # force a deterministic machine hostname regardless of the test host
+    monkeypatch.setattr(cap.socket, "gethostname", lambda: "buildbox-7")
+    monkeypatch.setattr(cap.platform, "node", lambda: "buildbox-7")
+    cap._HOSTNAME_RES = cap._hostname_patterns()
+    out = cap._scrub(
+        "ran on buildbox-7.local (bare buildbox-7); ns https://hc-proj.local/prov#; "
+        "repo hc-proj/app and settings.local.json"
+    )
+    assert "buildbox-7" not in out  # machine hostname redacted (bare + .local)
+    assert "hc-proj.local" not in out  # namespace host .local form redacted
+    assert "hc-proj/app" in out  # bare project token preserved (not over-redacted)
+    assert "settings.local.json" in out  # unrelated *.local untouched (no blanket rule)
+
+
 def test_example_uses_no_hardcoded_hmac_secret():
     # #F-048: the example must not carry a shippable, publicly-known HMAC secret a user could copy
     # into production. It derives a fresh random secret per run instead.

content.text:
Important distinction. Most of these matches are TRANSCRIPTS OF PRIOR AGENTS discussing/grepping for `[REDACTED]` — i.e. the literal string `[REDACTED]` appears in tool-call commands and reasoning text (e.g. an agent running `grep [REDACTED]`, or discussing the finding). The fix's `_hostname_patterns` only redacts `[REDACTED]` if `hc-grc` was supplied via `ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS`. 

But here's the real question: did the re-scrub actually run with `ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS=hc-grc` set? If it did, EVERY `[REDACTED]` should be `[REDACTED].local` or similar. The presence of 15 committed files with raw `[REDACTED]` shows the re-scrub did NOT redact them. Now I have strong evidence of a genuine residual leak. The string `[REDACTED]` appears as a real PROV-O RDF namespace `https://[REDACTED]/prov#` — exactly the "internal RDF-namespace host" the finding's docstring says ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS is meant to redact. This means one of two things: either the re-scrub was NOT run with `ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS=hc-grc`, or these files weren't re-scrubbed. Either way, the VERIFY claim "grep transcripts/ for [REDACTED] → 0" is FALSE. content.text:
So the live machine hostname is `[REDACTED]` — and indeed `[REDACTED]`/`[REDACTED]` greps return 0 in the corpus. That part of the fix (machine hostname redaction) clearly worked operationally. The bare-form redaction of `[REDACTED]` works because it's the machine name. 

Now let me confirm the test discriminates by reverting the hostname patterns mentally — the test asserts `buildbox-7` not in out, which requires `_hostname_patterns` to derive it. If `_HOSTNAME_RES` were empty (reverted), the test would fail. Let me verify by checking the over-redaction guard: a 3-char hostname like `mac` would be skipped (len<4). Good. I now need to be careful and fair. The committed file with the most genuine namespace leaks (`agent-ac587049a13085463.jsonl`, 9 occurrences) — let me check WHICH session this is. The session `8d54714a...` is the CURRENT review session (it contains pass-9 content including this very review's enqueue notifications dated 2026-06-17T16:03). That session was captured by the pre-commit hook at commit time.

The critical question for fairness: the finding says "The committed corpus was re-scrubbed" and "VERIFY ... [REDACTED] → 0". But these `[REDACTED]` occurrences are overwhelmingly inside transcripts of pass-9 agents who were *themselves analyzing the P9-PII-1 finding* — discussing the namespace, running greps for it, showing test output. These are meta-references generated DURING pass 9.

The decisive point: regardless of provenance, the committed corpus at HEAD contains 12 files with cleartext `[REDACTED]`, including the genuine RDF namespace `https://[REDACTED]/prov#`. The scrubber's `_hostname_patterns` WOULD redact these IF `ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS=hc-grc` were set when the hook ran. This confirms the residual gap. `ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS` is referenced ONLY in the test (with a synthetic `hc-proj`) and the scrubber's own code/docstring. It is NOT set anywhere in the pre-commit hook, no config file, no env wiring. 

So the `.local` redaction for the namespace host `[REDACTED]` is effectively dead at runtime: the pre-commit hook that does the actual scrubbing never sets `ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS`, so `[REDACTED]` is never redacted operationally. The 12 committed files prove it: the genuine RDF namespace `https://[REDACTED]/prov#` leaked through into the very commit that claims to fix this.

The machine-hostname half (`[REDACTED]` → bare+`.local`) works because it's auto-derived from `socket.gethostname()`. But the namespace-host half — the explicit `[REDACTED]` case named in the finding title — does NOT redact in production because the env var it depends on is never set.

content.text:
My findings are conclusive.

**Appropriate (root cause / approach / layer):** Yes. The scrubber is the right layer; `_hostname_patterns` correctly auto-derives the machine hostname (socket/platform/scutil) and redacts bare+`.local`, while namespace hosts get only their `.local` form redacted to preserve the bare project token. The `>=4` length guard and `\b` boundary sensibly prevent over-redacting short/common hostnames. The machine-hostname half is operationally proven: `[REDACTED]`/`[REDACTED]` are now 0 in the committed corpus. The test passes and discriminates (it monkeypatches `socket.gethostname`/`platform.node` and rebuilds `_HOSTNAME_RES`; if reverted to empty patterns, the `buildbox-7`/`hc-proj.local` assertions fail). Over-redaction guards are validated.

**Complete:** No — there is a real residual gap, and the finding's own VERIFY claim is false.

- The finding asserts "VERIFY: grep transcripts/ for ... [REDACTED] → 0" and "The committed corpus was re-scrubbed (grep now 0)." Both are FALSE. The committed HEAD corpus contains `[REDACTED]` in 12 files (74 line-hits), including the genuine internal RDF namespace `https://[REDACTED]/prov#` — precisely the leak class the docstring says ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS exists to scrub.
- Root cause of the residual: the namespace-host redaction depends entirely on `ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS`, but that env var is set NOWHERE operationally — not in the pre-commit hook, not in any config/wiring. It appears only in the test (synthetic `hc-proj`) and the code's own docstring. So the `.local` half of the fix is dead code at runtime; `[REDACTED]` is never redacted by the actual capture path.
- The machine-hostname half works only because it's auto-derived; the named target `[REDACTED]` from the finding title leaked straight into the commit that claims to fix it.

This is a CONCERN (not REJECTED): the code mechanism is correct and the machine-hostname portion genuinely fixes part of the finding, but the namespace-host portion has no runtime wiring and the corpus was not actually cleaned for `[REDACTED]`.

input.rationale: The fix is appropriate in approach and layer: scripts/capture_transcripts.py:_hostname_patterns() correctly auto-derives the machine hostname (socket.gethostname/platform.node/scutil) and redacts bare+.local, while ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS namespace hosts get only their .local form redacted to preserve the bare project token. The >=4 length guard plus \b boundary properly avoid over-redacting short/common hostnames. The machine-hostname half is operationally PROVEN: git grep HEAD for [REDACTED] and [REDACTED] both return 0 in the committed corpus. The regression test (tests/test_scripts.py::test_scrub_redacts_machine_and_namespace_hostnames) passes and DISCRIMINATES — it monkeypatches socket/platform and rebuilds _HOSTNAME_RES, so if the patterns were reverted to empty the buildbox-7 / hc-proj.local assertions would fail; it also asserts bare hc-proj/app and settings.local.json are preserved.\n\nBUT it is NOT complete. The finding's explicit VERIFY claim ('grep transcripts/ for [REDACTED] -> 0' and 'The committed corpus was re-scrubbed') is FALSE. git grep HEAD finds [REDACTED] in 12 committed files (74 line-hits), including the genuine internal RDF namespace https://[REDACTED]/prov# — exactly the leak class the docstring says ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS exists to scrub. Root cause of the residual: the namespace-host (.local) redaction depends entirely on the ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS env var, which is set NOWHERE operationally (not in .githooks/pre-commit, no config, no wiring) — it appears only in the test (synthetic 'hc-proj') and the code's own docstring. So the .local half of the fix is effectively dead at runtime, and the named target [REDACTED] leaked straight into the very commit that claims to fix it.
concerns: VERIFY claim false: [REDACTED] is NOT 0 in the committed corpus. git grep HEAD -- transcripts/ finds it in 12 files / 74 line-hits, including the genuine internal RDF namespace https://[REDACTED]/prov# in agent-ac587049a13085463.jsonl, agent-afd8f190045689443.jsonl, agent-a492eed5034215bde.jsonl, the top-level session jsonl, and workflow .json files.

Namespace-host (.local) redaction is unwired at runtime: ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS is referenced only in scripts/capture_transcripts.py (code+docstring) and tests/test_scripts.py (synthetic 'hc-proj'). It is set nowhere in the pre-commit hook or any config, so _hostname_patterns() never receives 'hc-grc' in production and never redacts [REDACTED]. The named target of the finding is therefore not redacted by the actual capture path.

Corpus was not actually re-scrubbed for [REDACTED]: the operator-internal mDNS/namespace hostname remains in cleartext across the committed pass-9 transcripts despite the commit message asserting 'Re-scrubbed corpus (grep now 0)'. The machine-hostname half ([REDACTED]) genuinely works only because it is auto-derived without needing the env var.

Forward fix incomplete unless ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS=hc-grc (or equivalent) is wired into the pre-commit capture path AND the existing corpus is re-run; otherwise every future commit will continue to leak [REDACTED].

content.text: I've completed the adversarial review of P9-PII-1 and returned a CONCERN verdict via StructuredOutput. Summary: the code approach is correct and the machine-hostname half is proven ([REDACTED] → 0 in the committed corpus, test discriminates), but the namespace-host half ([REDACTED]) is operationally dead — ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS is wired nowhere, so the finding's "grep → 0" VERIFY claim is false (12 committed files still contain [REDACTED], including the genuine RDF namespace https://[REDACTED]/prov#).
